mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, reducing recession risk and supporting equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings and consumer spending. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive 20-day forward returns when PAYEMS shows consistent month-over-month growth.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137754.0 (Nov 2013) reflects post-crisis recovery momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 2.5% in a tightening cycle, the cost of capital increases and equity discount rates compress future cash flows more aggressively, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 2.5% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.98% (Nov 2013) remains in accommodative zone
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 65% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) combined with accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand and construction activity, indicating late-cycle expansion. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive 20-day returns when both series show concurrent month-over-month acceleration, reflecting joint confirmation of demand strength.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase > 2% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1032.0 (Oct 2013) and RSAFS at 385448.0 (Nov 2013) show recovery momentum
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand acceleration
failure_condition: Interaction signal produces positive 20d returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or joint acceleration fails to outperform single-series signals (Rank IC difference < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month by more than 1%, signaling supply-side weakness and potential margin compression for cyclical firms. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 5-day forward returns as the market reprices earnings expectations downward in response to manufacturing weakness.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in < 55% of instances when INDPRO declines > 1%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.8% in a single month, signaling deteriorating labor market conditions and recession risk. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 60-day forward returns as equity investors reprice growth expectations and increase risk premiums in response to labor market deterioration.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in a single month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.5% (Nov 2013) remains below pre-crisis levels
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances when UNRATE spikes > 0.4pp, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 2.0%, creating a negative real rate environment that initially supports equity valuations through asset price inflation but signals future tightening risk. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive 20-day returns in the current low-rate regime but reverses when tightening begins.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 215.132 (Nov 2013) and FEDFUNDS at 1.98% indicate accommodative monetary stance
regime_hint: accommodative monetary regime with moderate inflation
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in < 52% of triggered instances, or the mechanism reverses direction (produces negative returns) when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5% in > 70% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows acceleration above 2.5% annualized rate, indicating robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive 60-day forward returns when GDP growth momentum is sustained above trend.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11701.9 billion (Q3 2013) reflects post-crisis recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive GDP growth fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 58% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both decelerate simultaneously (month-over-month growth < 0.5%), signaling synchronized supply and labor market weakness that compresses profit margins and reduces earnings growth expectations. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative 20-day returns when both indicators weaken together.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth < 0.5% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:Synchronized weakness in production and employment signals demand destruction
failure_condition: Negative 20d returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or joint deceleration fails to outperform single-series signals (Rank IC difference < 0.02).
